text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 11 March 2017 

Dr Priya Menon 
Consultant Neurologist  
North Thames Neurosciences Centre  
Royal Borough Hospital, 12 Kingfisher Way, London W6 3AB  

Dear Dr Priya Menon, 

Mr Adam Whitfield, DOB: 22-09-1983, Hospital No: RBH203944 NHS No. NHS-4482196701  
Flat 4, 27 Willow Court, Hammersmith, London W6 8PL  

Diagnosis: 

None Epilepsy – patient does not have epilepsy; previous events now considered non-epileptic and resolved. Seizure-free off ASMs since 06/07/2016. 

Aetiology: 

No structural, genetic, metabolic or immune cause identified. Prior episodes were consistent with stress-related non-epileptic events; no evidence of epileptiform activity on repeated EEGs and normal brain MRI. 

Other Medical Problems: 

Generalised anxiety symptoms markedly improved with guided self-help and sleep hygiene; mild seasonal allergic rhinitis. 

Management Plan: 

- No anti-seizure medications indicated. Seizure-free off ASMs since 06/07/2016.  
- Continue non-pharmacological strategies (sleep regularity, stress management).  
- No driving restrictions from a neurological/epilepsy perspective given current status, in line with guidance; advise to inform licensing authority if any new attacks occur.  
- Routine follow-up open appointment; patient to contact service if symptoms recur.  

Clinical Summary: 

Since a minor adjustment at the last visit—specifically focusing on regularising sleep and hydration while discontinuing a low-dose PRN benzodiazepine—the patient reports sustained improvement. Before this improvement, the recorded seizure frequency was fourteen/30, but careful review suggests these were non-epileptic stress-related episodes rather than epileptic seizures. There have been no further events, and the patient has been Seizure-free off ASMs since 06/07/2016. He reports normal work functioning, resumed exercise, and no episodes suggestive of syncope or dissociation. Neurological examination today is normal. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Clinic date is 11 March 2017. The letter repeatedly states the patient has been seizure-free off anti-seizure medications since 06/07/2016. Calculate duration from 06 July 2016 to 11 March 2017: July 6 to August 6 = 1 month, August 6 to September 6 = 2, September 6 to October 6 = 3, October 6 to November 6 = 4, November 6 to December 6 = 5, December 6 to January 6 = 6, January 6 to February 6 = 7, February 6 to March 6 = 8, plus 5 more days to March 11. Thus ≥8 months seizure-free. This meets the ≥6-month criterion for 'seizure free'.
